task_completion.description: Construct a scalene triangle ABC with vertices A(0, 0), B(6, 0), and C(2, 5)
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoGebra window, a triangle should be visible with vertices exactly at the coordinates (0,0), (6,0), and (2,5). These coordinates should be visible on the grid or in the Algebra view on the left.

task_completion.description: Construct equilateral triangles external to each side of ABC
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three new triangles should be attached to the outer edges of the base triangle. They must appear equilateral (all sides equal) and point outwards from the base triangle.

task_completion.description: Identify the centroids of the three external equilateral triangles
task_completion.visual_evidence: There should be distinct points placed exactly at the center (centroid) of each of the three external equilateral triangles. These might be labeled points in the graphics view or listed in the Algebra view.

task_completion.description: Form the 'Napoleon Triangle' by connecting the centroids
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new triangle should be drawn connecting the three centroid points identified in the previous step. This is the inner Napoleon Triangle.

task_completion.description: Include a dynamic measurement or text annotation verifying that the Napoleon Triangle is equilateral
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for distance measurements on the sides of the Napoleon Triangle or a text annotation in the GeoGebra view showing that all three sides are equal. The value should be approximately 5.91.

Look for the filename 'napoleon_theorem.ggb' in the GeoGebra window title bar, a 'Save' dialog box showing the path '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/', or a terminal/file manager verifying the file's existence in that directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to construct the theorem rather than generating a file via terminal scripts
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application open with visible tool selections, mouse movements, or commands being typed into the GeoGebra input bar.

integrity.description: The agent used actual geometric relationships rather than manually placing independent points to fake the result
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should show dependent objects (e.g., Polygons, Segments, Intersects, or Centroids) rather than just independent points with hardcoded coordinates for the centroids.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write a text file or use an image editor to fake the final output
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must clearly be the functional GeoGebra application, with the standard toolbars, grid, and Algebra sidebar present and matching the construction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base triangle must have coordinates exactly at [[0, 0], [6, 0], [2, 5]]. The correct side length of the resulting Napoleon triangle is approximately 5.91 units (tolerance 0.1). The expected filename is 'napoleon_theorem.ggb'.
